claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, who's 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex. I have a few more questions to help determine your eligibility.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Are you responsible for Alex** – meaning you pay for things like his food, clothes, and accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I pay for all his food, clothes, and everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you for confirming that.

**Is Alex in full-time education, or has he left education/training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's still in school - he's only 15, so he's in secondary school doing his GCSEs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, that's helpful.

Just a couple more questions:

**Are you and Alex living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules, I now have everything I need to make a determination about your Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

- ✅ **Alex is under 16** (he's 15 years old)
- ✅ **You live in the UK** (Manchester)
- ✅ **Alex lives with you**
- ✅ **You are responsible for him** (you pay for his food, clothes, and other needs)
- ✅ **No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him**

You can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. It's worth noting that even when Alex turns 16, you may continue to receive Child Benefit as long as he stays in approved education or training, up until he turns 20.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16. He lives with his parent in the UK (Manchester), making the parent responsible for him. The parent pays for Alex's food, clothes, and other necessities. Alex is in full-time education (secondary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the core eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, lives in the UK, the claimant is responsible for him, and he lives with the claimant.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They are responsible for one child (Alex, aged 15) who lives with them in the UK. Alex is under 16 and in full-time education. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. There are no disqualifying factors present.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16. He lives with his parent in the UK (Manchester), making the parent responsible for him. The parent pays for Alex's food, clothes, and other necessities. Alex is in full-time education (secondary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the core eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, lives in the UK, the claimant is responsible for him, and he lives with the claimant.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They are responsible for one child (Alex, aged 15) who lives with them in the UK. Alex is under 16 and in full-time education. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. There are no disqualifying factors present.
